entities.name: Liberal Arts

attributes.categories: Grammar, Rhetoric, Logic, Arithmetic, Geometry, Music, Astronomy

entities.raw_quote: the knowledge of every Science, Art, and Wisdom; and of every Faculty of Memory... attaining to them in one Moneth

entities.name: General Abilities
entities.raw_quote: the first of these deals with what the author calls 'generals'; these are abilities of broad application – memory, eloquence, understanding and perseverance

entities.name: Special Abilities
entities.raw_quote: The second section deals with the 'specials', giving prayers in sequence for each of the Liberal Arts

entities.name: Prayer in the Beginning

attributes.content_summary: Trinitarian invocation asking for illumination and knowledge

entities.raw_quote: In the Name of the Holy and undivided Trinity, beginneth this most Holy Art of Knowledge... I humbly implore and beseech, that thou wilt mercifully with the Father, illustrate my Minde

entities.name: Theological Sciences
entities.raw_quote: in the third place we come to speak of Theologie; which Sciences thou shalt attain to, by the Operations of these Orations

relationships.to: Hely Scemath Amazaz Hemel Sathusteon hheli Tamazam
Hely Scemath Amazaz Hemel Sathusteon hheli Tamazam

summary: This chunk introduces the Ars Notoria as an angelic art revealed to Solomon by God through His angels, emphasizing its purpose of acquiring knowledge of liberal arts and spiritual faculties. It outlines the structure (generals/specials), mentions key figures (Solomon, Apollonius, Turner), describes divine names and prayers (e.g., Hely Scemath, Theos Megale, Splendor of Wisdom), and introduces ritual elements like Notes and Triumphal Figures tied to lunar timing. The text stresses the mystical nature of these elements and their reliance on faith, reverence, and proper timing.
key_passages: The Notory Art revealed by the Most High Creator to Solomon, which the Most High Creator by his Holy Angels ministered to Solomon upon the Alter of the Temple; that thereby in short time he knew all Arts and Sciences...

Hely, Scemath Amazaz, Hemel, Sathusteon, hheli Tamazam, &cet. which Solomon entitled, His First Revelation; and that to be without any Interpretation: It being a Science of so Transcendent a purity...

That is the first Figure of the Notory Art, which is manifestly sited upon a Quadrangle Note: And this is Angelical Wisdom... and in the Notory Art it is written, To be the Foundation of the whole Science.
There are also certain Figures or Orations which Solomon in Chaldeack called, Hely; that is, Triumphal Orations of the Liberal Arts...

This Oration hath no Exposition in the Latine... because no Man could attain to the perfection thereof: and it was left so spiritual, because the Angel that declared it to Solomon, laid an inexcusable prohibition upon it...
